verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member that localizes to the cytosol in healthy cells and translocates to the mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis. Its entire function is intracellular — mediating mitochondrial outer-membrane permeabilization and cytochrome c release. BAX is not membrane-anchored, not secreted, and has no documented plasma-membrane localization or surface-accessibility mechanism.
